content: The frequency of oscillation of the spring mass system is
explanation:
$$ \begin{aligned} &\text { For the given spring mass system, }\\ &k_{\mathrm{eq}}=k+2 k+k=4 k \end{aligned} $$

$$ \text { ∴ Frequency, } \eta=\frac{1}{2 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{k_{\text {eq }}}{m}}=\frac{1}{2 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{4 k}{m}} $$

content: Five identical springs are used in the three configurations as shown in figure. The time periods of vertical oscillations in configurations (a), (b) and (c) are in the ratio.
explanation:
To determine the time periods of vertical oscillations in different configurations of springs, we examine each setup.

Configuration (a):

The time period, $ T_a $, is given by the formula:

$ T_{a} = 2\pi \sqrt{\frac{m}{k}} $

Configuration (b):

In this setup, two identical springs are arranged in series, effectively halving the spring constant. Thus, the time period, $ T_b $, is:

$ T_{b} = 2\pi \sqrt{\frac{m}{\frac{k}{2}}} = 2\pi \sqrt{\frac{2m}{k}} $

Configuration (c):

In this setup, two identical springs are arranged in parallel, effectively doubling the spring constant. Hence, the time period, $ T_c $, is:

$ T_{c} = 2\pi \sqrt{\frac{m}{2k}} $

Now, calculating the ratios of these time periods, we get:

$ T_{a} : T_{b} : T_{c} = 1 : \sqrt{2} : \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} $

This ratio expresses the relative oscillation times for each configuration of the springs.

content: The $$x$$-$$t$$ graph of a particle performing simple harmonic motion is shown in the figure. The acceleration of the particle at $$t=2. \mathrm{~s}$$ is
explanation:
The motion of the particle performing S.H.M. represented by

$$\begin{aligned} x & =A \sin \omega t=1 \sin \frac{2 \pi}{T} \cdot t=\sin \frac{2 \pi}{8} t \\ \therefore \quad x & =\sin \left(\frac{\pi t}{4}\right) \end{aligned}$$

$$\therefore$$ Velocity, $$v=\frac{d x}{d t}$$

$$=\frac{d}{d t} \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) t=\frac{\pi}{4} \cos \left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) t$$

$$\therefore$$ Acceleration of the particle.

$$\begin{aligned} a & =\frac{d v}{d t}=\frac{d}{d t}\left[\frac{\pi}{4} \cos \left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) t\right] \\ & =-\frac{\pi}{4} \cdot \frac{\pi}{4} \cdot \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{4} t\right) \Rightarrow a=-\frac{\pi^2}{16} \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{4} \cdot t\right) \end{aligned}$$

At $$t=2 \mathrm{~s}$$

$$a=-\frac{\pi^2}{16} \cdot \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{4} \cdot 2\right)=-\frac{\pi^2}{16} \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}$$

content:
A tray of mass (M) 12 kg is supported by a spring as shown in the figure.

When the tray is pressed down and released, it executes SHM with a period of 1.5 s. When a block of mass m placed on the tray, the period of SHM changes to 3.0 s. The mass of block is
explanation:
Let k be the force constant of spring.

Time period, $$T = 2\pi \sqrt {{M \over k}} $$ ...... (i)

When block of mass m is placed in the tray, the time period of oscillations becomes

$$T' = 2\pi \sqrt {{{M + m} \over k}} $$ ...... (ii)

On dividing Eq. (ii) by Eq. (i), we get

$$ \Rightarrow {{T'} \over T} = {{\sqrt {M + m} } \over {\sqrt M }}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {3 \over {1.5}} = \sqrt {{{12 + m} \over {12}}} $$ [given, M = 12 kg, T = 1.5 s, T' = 3 s]

$$ \Rightarrow \sqrt {{{12 + m} \over {12}}} = 2$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ 12 + m = 48

$$\Rightarrow$$ m = 36 kg

content: A particle executes SHM with a frequency f. The frequency with which its kinetic energy oscillates is
explanation:
Let displacement, x = A$$\omega$$ sin $$\omega$$t

Velocity, $$v = {{dx} \over {dt}} = A\omega \cos \omega t$$

Kinetic energy of particle in SHM,

$$K = {1 \over 2}m{v^2} \Rightarrow K = {1 \over 2}m{\omega ^2}{A^2}{\cos ^2}\omega t$$

$$ = {1 \over 2}m{\omega ^2}{A^2}\left( {{{1 + \cos 2\omega t} \over 2}} \right)$$ [$$\because$$ $${\cos ^2}\theta = {{1 + \cos 2\theta } \over 2}$$]

$$ = {1 \over 4}m{\omega ^2}{A^2}(1 + \cos 2\omega t)$$

$$\therefore$$ New angular frequency, $${\omega _K} = 2\omega $$

$$\therefore$$ Frequency of oscillation of kinetic energy = 2f [$$\because$$ $$f = {\omega \over {2\pi }}$$]

content:
A simple pendulum is placed inside a lift, the lift is moving with a uniform acceleration. If the time periods of the pendulum while the lift is moving upwards and downwards are in the ratio of 1 : 3, then the acceleration of the lift is

[Take acceleration due to gravity, g = 10 m/s2]
explanation:
Let a be the acceleration of the lift.

Given, the ratio of time periods of pendulum while the lift is moving upwards and downwards,

T1 : T2 = 1 : 3

When lift is moving upwards, then total time period,

$${T_1} = 2\pi \sqrt {{I \over {g + a}}} $$........ (i)

When lift is moving downwards, then total time period,

$${T_2} = 2\pi \sqrt {{I \over {g - a}}} $$ ....... (ii)

Dividing Eq. (i) by Eq. (ii), we get

$${{{T_1}} \over {{T_2}}} = \sqrt {{{g - a} \over {g + a}}} \Rightarrow {1 \over 3} = \sqrt {{{g - a} \over {g + a}}} $$

or, $${{g - a} \over {g + a}} = {\left( {{1 \over 3}} \right)^2}$$ or, $${{g - a} \over {g + a}} = {1 \over 9}$$

$$ \Rightarrow 9g - 9a = g + a$$

$$ \Rightarrow 9g - g = 9a + a$$

$$ \Rightarrow 10a = 8g$$

$$a = {{8g} \over {10}} = {{8 \times 10} \over {10}} = 8$$ m/s2
